system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet section (straight duct along x-axis)
    // Vertices 0-7
    (-50  25  25)   // 0
    (-50  25 -25)   // 1
    (-50 -25 -25)   // 2
    (-50 -25  25)   // 3

    (  0  25  25)   // 4
    (  0  25 -25)   // 5
    (  0 -25 -25)   // 6
    (  0 -25  25)   // 7

    // Block 1 - bend section
    // Vertices 8-11
    ( 25   0  25)   // 8
    ( 25   0 -25)   // 9
    ( 25 -50 -25)   // 10
    ( 25 -50  25)   // 11

    // Block 2 - outlet straight duct
    // Vertices 12-15
    ( 25  -75  25)   // 12
    ( 25  -75 -25)   // 13
    ( 25 -125 -25)   // 14
    ( 25 -125  25)   // 15

    (-500  -75  25)   // 16
    (-500  -75 -25)   // 17
    (-500 -125 -25)   // 18
    (-500 -125  25)   // 19
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: inlet straight section
    hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: bend section
    hex (7 8 9 6 3 11 10 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: outlet straight section
    hex (12 16 17 13 15 19 18 14) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (16 19 18 17)
        );
    }

    walls
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 walls
            (0 4 5 1)
            (3 7 6 2)
            (0 4 7 3)
            (1 5 6 2)

            // Block 1 walls
            (7 8 9 6)
            (3 11 10 2)
            (7 3 11 8)
            (6 2 10 9)
            (8 9 10 11)

            // Block 2 walls
            (12 16 17 13)
            (15 19 18 14)
            (12 16 15 19)
            (13 17 18 14)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

{

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        "(k|epsilon)"   1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_A_Amodels/runs/squareBend/2/system/blockMeshDict/blocks at line 49.

    From function void Foam::blockDescriptor::check(const Foam::Istream&)
    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 100.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` â€” Block 0 is "inside-out"

**Error Message:**
```
Block hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) ... is inside-out
```

This means the vertex ordering in the first hex block definition does not follow the right-hand rule consistently, resulting in a negative volume.

### Root Cause

The issue is with the **vertex ordering** in the hex block definitions. In OpenFOAM's `blockMesh`, a hex block is defined as two faces: `(bottom face) (top face)`, where each face lists four vertices in counterclockwise order when viewed from outside the block (looking toward the opposite face).

Looking at the current block definitions:

- **Block 0:** `hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2)` â€” This mixes vertices from different sides incorrectly.
- **Block 1:** `hex (7 8 9 6 3 11 10 2)` â€” Same problem.
- **Block 2:** `hex (12 16 17 13 15 19 18 14)` â€” Same problem.

The vertices need to be reordered so that:
- Face 1 (bottom): vertices ordered CCW when viewed from below
- Face 2 (top): vertices ordered CCW when viewed from above
- And the bottom-to-top direction follows the right-hand rule (normal pointing out of face 1)

Additionally, looking at the **boundary faces**, there are inconsistencies between the block face definitions and the actual boundary patches. For example, the outlet face `(16 19 18 17)` doesn't match what the blocks actually define.

### Recommended Solution

Redesign the **entire block topology** following the proven squareBend tutorial pattern. The standard approach for a square bend uses these key principles:

1. **Reorder vertices per block** to ensure correct winding. For Block 0, the correct hex should likely be something like:
   ```
   hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)
   ```
   But more importantly, study the actual squareBend tutorial's blockMeshDict carefully and replicate its exact vertex positions and block connectivity.

2. **Fix the outlet face definition.** Looking at the block 2 vertices, the outlet should be at z=-125 plane (faces involving vertices 14, 13, 17, 18). The current outlet face `(16 19 18 17)` is at z=+25/-25, which is wrong.

3. **Simplify the geometry.** The current design tries to make three separate blocks sharing edges at single vertices (e.g., vertex 2 is shared by all three blocks). Consider redesigning so blocks share full faces rather than just edges/vertices, or restructure the entire mesh to avoid degenerate blocks.

---

## Error 2: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` â€” Cannot find "points" file

This is a **cascading error** â€” since `blockMesh` failed, no mesh was generated, so `rhoSimpleFoam` cannot find the mesh files (`points`, `faces`, `cells`, etc.).

**Resolution:** Fix the `blockMesh` error first, then re-run `rhoSimpleFoam`.

---

## Additional Issues Noted (from the rest of the setup)

While not causing the immediate errors, there are several boundary condition and patch name mismatches that will cause problems once the mesh is fixed:

1. **Boundary Patch Names Mismatch**: The `blockMeshDict` defines patches `inlet`, `outlet`, and `walls`. However, the `0/` field files (T, U, p) reference patches like `innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall` â€” these don't exist in the mesh. After `blockMesh` succeeds, only `inlet`, `outlet`, and `walls` patches will exist. All `0/` files must use exactly these three patch names.

2. **Pressure Boundary Condition**: The `p` file sets `outlet` to `fixedValue` with value 100000 Pa, but `controlDict` specifies `pressureInletOutletVelocity` at the outlet. These must be consistent â€” either change the outlet BC type in `p` to `zeroGradient` (recommended for OpenFOAM's rhoSimpleFoam) or change the outlet BC type in `controlDict` to `fixedValue`.

3. **Temperature Initial Field**: The `T` file initializes everything to 300 K, but the inlet is set to 1500 K. This is fine â€” the solution will adjust â€” but note the internal field doesn't matter much since the inlet BC overrides it.

4. **Alphat Wall Function Values**: The `alphat` file sets `value uniform 0` on walls, but the `k` and `epsilon` files have non-zero wall values. For consistency with the kEpsilon model, consider setting appropriate non-zero values or using `zeroGradient` on walls for `k` and `epsilon` (the standard squareBend tutorial does this).

---

## Summary of Actions

1. **Fix `blockMeshDict`**: Redefine the hex block vertex orderings to be geometrically valid (right-hand rule), fix the outlet face, and ensure proper patch definitions. Study the actual squareBend tutorial's blockMeshDict closely.

2. **Fix boundary patch names in all `0/` files**: Change `innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall` to match the mesh patches (`walls`), or restructure the mesh to have those named patches.

3. **Fix the outlet pressure BC**: Either use `zeroGradient` at the outlet in `p` (consistent with `pressureInletOutletVelocity`) or change the outlet BC type in `controlDict`.

4. **After fixing blockMesh**, run `blockMesh`, then `rhoSimpleFoam`.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Replace Block 0 hex definition from hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) to hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) ensuring bottom face 0-1-2-3 is CCW when viewed from below and top face 4-5-6-7 is CCW when viewed from above; Replace Block 1 hex definition from hex (7 8 9 6 3 11 10 2) to hex (4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11) with appropriate vertex positions for the bend section; Replace Block 2 hex definition from hex (12 16 17 13 15 19 18 14) to hex (12 13 14 15 16 17 18 19) ensuring correct winding; Update outlet boundary face from (16 19 18 17) to (16 17 18 19) matching the new block 2 vertex ordering; Remove innerWall outerWall lowerWall upperWall patches from boundary section and replace with a single walls patch containing all non-inlet non-outlet faces from all three blocks'}, {'file': '0/T', 'changes': 'Remove innerWall outerWall lowerWall upperWall entries from boundaryField; Add walls patch entry with type zeroGradient'}, {'file': '0/U', 'changes': 'Remove innerWall outerWall lowerWall upperWall entries from boundaryField; Add walls patch entry with type noSlip'}, {'file': '0/p', 'changes': 'Remove innerWall outerWall lowerWall upperWall entries from boundaryField; Add walls patch entry with type zeroGradient'}, {'file': '0/k', 'changes': 'Remove innerWall outerWall lowerWall upperWall entries from boundaryField; Add walls patch entry with type kqRWallFunction and value uniform 1'}, {'file': '0/epsilon', 'changes': 'Remove innerWall outerWall lowerWall upperWall entries from boundaryField; Add walls patch entry with type epsilonWallFunction and value uniform 200'}, {'file': '0/nut', 'changes': 'Remove innerWall outerWall lowerWall upperWall entries from boundaryField; Add walls patch entry with type nutkWallFunction and value uniform 0'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1500. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
